preamble: Vocalization of [f] can result in the mid-back rounded vowel [o], particularly when the labial element simplifies through a diphthongal stage during the transition from Latin to Romance.

examples.note: Path: [v/w] > [u] > [o]. NOTE: this example shows [v]→[o] vocalization, not [f]→[o]. The file may be mislabeled; the vocalization of labial fricatives/glides to [o] involves /v/ or /w/, not /f/.
